issues.title: Pay Applications page missing AIA G702/G703 PDF generation
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA criterion 7 and 8 for Payment Applications require pixel-perfect AIA G702 and G703 PDF generation. The page currently has no PDF export functionality. This is a P0 dealbreaker from PRODUCTION_ROADMAP (P1-1). GCs cannot bill owners without these forms.
issues.prompt: In the file src/pages/PayApplications.tsx, in the main PayApplications component: Add a 'Generate G702/G703 PDF' button in the pay app detail view header area. BEFORE: The pay app detail view shows line items and totals but has no export/PDF generation capability. AFTER: Add a button with icon (FileText from lucide-react) labeled 'Generate AIA G702/G703' that calls a new service function. For now, wire it to show a toast 'PDF generation coming soon' using the existing toast pattern. Add the button only when the pay app status is 'submitted' or 'approved'. Use theme.colors.primaryOrange for the button background, white text, borderRadius.md, and padding spacing['2'] spacing['4']. Hide the button for users without financials.edit permission by checking usePermissions(). After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: SCurve chart lacks accessible data table fallback and proper ARIA roles
issues.description: The SVG chart references aria-describedby='scurve-data-table' but no such table exists in the DOM. Screen reader users get no data. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards require axe-core zero critical violations. The keyboard navigation exists but announcements use a state variable that is never rendered to an aria-live region.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/budget/SCurve.tsx, in the SCurve component: BEFORE: The component has aria-describedby='scurve-data-table' but no element with that id exists. The 'announcement' state is set but never rendered to an aria-live region. AFTER: 1) Add a visually hidden table after the SVG with id='scurve-data-table' that lists months as rows with columns for Planned ($M) and Actual ($M) using the plannedData and actualData arrays. Style it with position: 'absolute', width: 1, height: 1, overflow: 'hidden', clip: 'rect(0,0,0,0)'. 2) Add a div with role='status' and aria-live='polite' also visually hidden (same clip style) that renders the 'announcement' state value so screen readers announce data point changes on arrow key navigation. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Lien waiver tracking page not wired to real data with proper workflow states
issues.description: The autoGenerateLienWaivers function is imported but the four AIA lien waiver types (conditional/unconditional progress/final) are defined as types but never enforced in the creation flow. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA for Pay Apps criterion 10 requires lien waiver integration blocking pay app submission when waivers are missing. This validation does not exist.
issues.prompt: In the file src/api/endpoints/payApplications.ts, find the section where pay applications are submitted (status change to 'submitted'). BEFORE: Pay apps can be submitted without any lien waiver validation. The LIEN_WAIVER_TYPES constant and LienWaiverType type are defined but unused in submission logic. AFTER: Add a validation function called validateLienWaiversForSubmission that: 1) Queries the lien_waivers table for the given pay_application_id, 2) Checks that at least one conditional_progress waiver exists per subcontractor who has line items in this pay period, 3) Returns an object { valid: boolean, missingWaivers: Array<{ vendor_name: string, waiver_type: LienWaiverType }> }. Export this function so the frontend can call it before submission and display warnings. Do NOT block submission yet (just warn), since this is advisory. Use the existing supabase client import and transformSupabaseError pattern. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Waterfall chart and Treemap are not touch-friendly on mobile
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Budget criterion 13 requires charts to remain interactive with pinch-to-zoom on mobile. The WaterfallChart uses hover events (onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave) that do not fire on touch devices. Touch targets on mobile bars are below the 44px Apple HIG minimum. The isMobile detection uses window.innerWidth directly which causes hydration issues.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/budget/WaterfallChart.tsx, in the WaterfallChart component: BEFORE: Bar interactions use onMouseEnter/onMouseLeave which do not work on touch. Mobile bars have no minimum touch target height. Hovering is the only way to see values. AFTER: 1) Add onTouchStart handler to each bar div that sets hoveredBar to that bar's label (same as onMouseEnter), and add onTouchEnd that clears it after 2 seconds via setTimeout. 2) In the mobile horizontal bar layout, set minHeight to 44 on each bar container div to meet Apple HIG touch target. 3) Always show the value label (fmt(bar.value)) next to each bar on mobile (when isMobile is true) instead of only on hover, using a span with fontSize typography.fontSize.xs, color colors.textSecondary, positioned after the bar. 4) Add aria-label to each bar div with the format '[label]: [formatted value]'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Earned Value Dashboard shows empty state too eagerly and lacks benchmark comparisons
issues.description: PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Budget criterion 2 requires variance shown as both dollar and percentage with severity colors. The EV dashboard shows an empty state when budgetItems is empty but does not guide users to create budget items. It also lacks industry benchmark comparison (CPI/SPI of 1.0 baseline) which CFOs expect. The metric cards truncate at mobile widths.
issues.prompt: In the file src/components/budget/EarnedValueDashboard.tsx, in the EarnedValueDashboard component: BEFORE: Empty state says 'Set up your budget to see earned value metrics' with no actionable CTA button. Metric cards use a 3-column grid that breaks on narrow screens. No benchmark reference line for CPI/SPI = 1.0. AFTER: 1) In the empty state div, add a button element styled with background colors.primaryOrange, color 'white', border 'none', borderRadius borderRadius.md, padding spacing['2'] + ' ' + spacing['4'], cursor 'pointer', fontSize typography.fontSize.body, fontWeight typography.fontWeight.semibold. The button text should be 'Set Up Budget'. Add an onClick that navigates using window.location.hash = '#budget-setup' (or use the app's navigation pattern if available). 2) Change the grid from METRIC_GRID ('repeat(3, 1fr)') to use a responsive approach: add a style check where if window.innerWidth < 768, use 'repeat(2, 1fr)', and if < 480, use '1fr'. Since this is inline styles, compute it in a useMemo or local variable. 3) For each EVMetric card that shows CPI or SPI, add a small text line below the value: '1.0 = on target' in fontSize typography.fontSize.xs, color colors.textTertiary. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors.

invented_features.title: Cash Flow Forecast Engine
invented_features.description: 13-week forward-looking cash flow projection chart built from committed costs, approved pay apps, pending COs, and historical collection speed. Shows inflows (billings) vs outflows (sub payments) with net cash position line. Alerts when a cash gap is projected. This is the #1 feature CFOs request and no competitor does it natively. Would live in src/components/budget/CashFlowForecast.tsx using the existing budget data hooks and computeProjectFinancials from financialEngine.ts.

competitive_intel: Procore's financial module requires manual Excel uploads for job costing and has no earned value analysis. Their pay app workflow lacks AIA G702/G703 PDF generation, forcing PMs to use external tools like Textura. SiteSync's financial engine already has computeCurrentPaymentDue with proper AIA line calculations and an earned value dashboard, which is ahead of Procore's offering. However, the lack of PDF export and lien waiver enforcement means the workflow is incomplete. Sage Intacct integration (P0-1 in roadmap) would be the killer differentiator since Procore's accounting integrations are widely criticized as unreliable.
summary: The financial engine has strong foundational domain logic (AIA G702 formulas, earned value calculations, waterfall charts) but critical gaps prevent production use. The top priorities are: (1) AIA G702/G703 PDF generation is completely absent, which is the P1-1 roadmap item and a dealbreaker for every GC; (2) accessibility violations in charts (missing data tables, broken aria-describedby references) would fail any compliance audit; (3) lien waiver validation on pay app submission is defined but not enforced, leaving a major construction workflow gap; (4) mobile touch interactions on financial charts are non-functional since they rely on mouse hover events; (5) the earned value dashboard needs responsive grid and actionable empty states. The module scores 53 overall, up from prior cycles due to real-time subscriptions and solid TypeScript typing, but needs PDF generation and lien waiver enforcement to reach 70+.
